title: psyllium indicum
intro: Psyllium indicum, also known as Plantago ovata or Indian psyllium, is a plant species that belongs to the plantain family and is native to the Indian subcontinent. The medicinal properties of Psyllium indicum include its ability to absorb water, forming a gel-like substance that can help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes. The health benefits of Psyllium indicum include its use in treating constipation, diarrhea, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve blood sugar control. Psyllium indicum can be prepared as a herbal supplement in capsule or powder form, or as a fiber-rich breakfast cereal, making it an easily accessible and convenient addition to a healthy diet.
